genre_refs: John C. McRae engraving 'Washington Praying at Valley Forge'; religious-patriotic Americana
description: A designed black-and-white devotional/patriotic graphic built from the antique engraving 'George Washington praying at Valley Forge' by John C. McRae (credited in the tweet). The desaturated, high-contrast image shows General George Washington kneeling in prayer in a dark wooded winter setting, hands clasped, his tricorn hat and cape beside him; a small figure (an aide) watches from behind a rock at left. The Department of Labor has added a designed bottom banner in an ornate white blackletter/Old English serif font reading 'One Nation Under God', turning a historical artwork into a shareable inspirational meme card. The grayscale treatment and gothic typography give it a solemn, reverent, almost ecclesiastical tone. Messaging technique: civil-religion nationalism fusing Christianity, the Founding, and 'America's 250th year' (per the tweet 'America will Prevail'). Target audience: religious conservatives and patriotic followers; intent is to associate the federal Labor Department's account with faith-and-country sentiment rather than any labor policy.
